description_long: This is a polearm-style axe featuring a long, cylindrical handle with a dark, wood-like finish and metallic bands spaced along its length. The head of the axe is composed of a large, black, double-edged blade with a pronounced angular design, including a main cutting edge and a secondary, smaller edge opposite it. A sharp, spear-like spike extends from the top, making the weapon suitable for both slashing and thrusting. The blade is mounted perpendicular to the handle, with a robust socket securing it in place. The overall design is reminiscent of a medieval halberd, combining both axe and spear functionalities. The object appears sturdy and functional, with no visible decorative engravings or markings.
description: A long-handled, black-bladed poleaxe with a spear tip and angular double-edged head, featuring a dark wooden shaft reinforced by metal bands.
description_view_0: The axe is shown with its broad, angular blade and spear tip clearly visible, highlighting the double-edged design and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle, with the spear tip prominent.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the axe head is visible, displaying the secondary edge and the full handle, similar to view 0 but mirrored.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, mirroring view 1, emphasizing the narrowness of the blade and the length of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical handle made of dark wood, reinforced with evenly spaced metal bands, smooth surface, round cross-section, no visible grip texture.
attribute_blade_design: Large, angular double-edged blade with a pronounced main edge and a smaller secondary edge, black finish, integrated spear-like spike at the top, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional socket mounting with the blade fitted perpendicular to the handle and reinforced by metal bands.
